title: messages: deserialize_file_name does not reject null bytes
description: deserialize_file_name rejects '/', '\\', '..' and empty names but does not reject null bytes (\x00). A hostile peer can send name 'evil\x00.txt', which passes validation. On POSIX, tokio::fs::write with a path containing \x00 will error, but the error path just logs a warning and does not surface to the user. On Windows, the OS would truncate at \x00 resulting in the name 'evil' and potentially overwriting an existing file. Fix: reject any name containing a null byte.
